content.claim: Nearly 18% of Hungary's population lived in the capital, almost 53% in towns, and 30% in villages as of 1 January 2024; migration gains in villages declined significantly from 2023 onward, reversing a pandemic-era counter-urbanization trend.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Regional sub-variation within villages category not disaggregated
Short-term migration volatility may not reflect long-term structural settlement patterns
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
